title: Add Reporting Filter to Dictionary Screen
story: As a DataTools Pro user on the Dictionary screen, I want to filter fields by their Reporting status instead of Migration status, so that I can quickly identify which fields are relevant to reporting.
description: On the Dictionary screen, the existing 'filter by migrate' option was replaced with a 'filter by Reporting' option. The filter is context-aware: when the user is on the Dictionary view, the Reporting filter is displayed. When toggling to the Migration view, migration-specific filters load and the dictionary filter state resets. This prevents cross-view filter contamination — filtering by Reporting on Dictionary and then switching to Migration reloads all migration data without carrying over the dictionary filter.
acceptance_criteria: Dictionary screen displays a 'Filter by Reporting' dropdown instead of 'Filter by Migrate'
Selecting 'Yes' on the Reporting filter shows only fields flagged for reporting
Switching from Dictionary to Migration resets data and shows migration-specific filters
Switching from Migration to Dictionary resets data and shows the Reporting filter
Filter state does not persist across view toggles
ux_changes: Replaced the migration filter with a Reporting filter on the Dictionary view. Each view (Dictionary, Migration) now loads its own context-specific filter set.
middleware_changes: Filter logic was updated to detect the active view (Dictionary vs Migration) and serve the appropriate filter options. View toggling triggers a data reset.
implementation_notes: The implementation detects which tab is active and dynamically shows/hides the appropriate filter. Switching views triggers a full data reload rather than attempting to reconcile filter state across views.
comment_summary: Developer implemented view-specific filters that reset on toggle. Product owner confirmed the behavior matches expectations and closed the ticket.
